title: PaymentIntent
description: In-flight payment intent objects — mirrors provider intent lifecycle from initialization → confirmation → success/failure/cancelled. 3DS2 step-up flows live here. `client_secret` + `provider_reference_id` encrypted at rest. Carries `tenant_id` only. NEVER carries `application_id`, `region_id`, `organization_id`, `branch_id`, or `scope_node_id`.

invoice_id.description: Parent invoice. RESTRICT — invoices with active intents cannot be voided until intents terminate.

provider.description: Enum: stripe / paddle / adyen / square / paypal / custom_webhook.

provider_reference_id.description: ENCRYPTED AT REST. Provider-native intent: pi_... (Stripe), txn_... (Paddle), session-id (Adyen), payment-id (Square), order_... (PayPal). Immutable after create.

amount_cents.description: The amount to capture (in cents). Frozen at intent create.

currency.description: ISO 4217 3-letter code. Frozen at intent create.

status.description: Enum: initialized / requires_action / requires_payment_method / requires_confirmation / requires_capture / processing / succeeded / cancelled / failed. State machine one-way — enforced by observer.

capture_method.description: Enum: automatic (capture on authorization — default) / manual (capture later via POST /payments/{payment}/capture — requires payment_manual_capture entitlement, Medium+).

confirmation_method.description: Enum: automatic (provider auto-confirms after method attach) / manual (customer explicitly confirms via SDK). manual is required for 3DS2 flows.

next_action_type.description: Enum: redirect_to_url / use_stripe_sdk / three_d_secure_redirect / verify_with_micro_deposits / oxxo_display_details. Set when status='requires_action'. Instructs the customer's SDK how to complete the step-up flow.

next_action_url.description: Redirect URL for step-up flows. Set for redirect_to_url + three_d_secure_redirect.

next_action_data.description: Provider-specific step-up payload. Stripe's `next_action.use_stripe_sdk.stripe_js`; Adyen's `action.paymentData`; PayPal's approvalUrl. NEVER contains PAN — just SDK invocation params.

payment_method_id.description: The saved method the customer attached (nullable — one-time methods, first-time cards). Attached during requires_payment_method → requires_confirmation transition. RESTRICT — historical intents preserve the method reference.

client_secret.description: ENCRYPTED AT REST. Provider client-side confirmation secret (Stripe pi_..._secret_...; Adyen paymentData; PayPal orderID). Passed to the customer's provider SDK. Encryption key ≠ provider_reference_id encryption key — defense-in-depth.

last_error_code.description: Provider error code on the most recent failure. Stripe: 'card_declined', 'authentication_required', etc.

last_error_message.description: Human-readable error message. Truncated to 512 chars.

last_error_type.description: Enum: authentication_error / card_error / api_error / idempotency_error / rate_limit_error / validation_error. Nullable when no error occurred.

succeeded_at.description: When status transitioned to 'succeeded'. Once set, IMMUTABLE.

cancelled_at.description: When status transitioned to 'cancelled'. Once set, IMMUTABLE.

provider_metadata.description: ENCRYPTED AT REST. Full provider intent response for debugging. Redacted from support-tool views.

indexes.name: payment_intents_provider_reference_idx
indexes.description: For webhook dispatch lookup — the webhook body carries provider_reference_id; we join on it. Note: encrypted column requires a matching hash column for exact-match index in production; here we assume the query goes through the SearchableEncrypted trait.

x-invariants: state transitions are one-way per state machine: initialized → {requires_action, requires_payment_method, requires_confirmation, processing}; requires_action → {requires_confirmation, requires_capture, processing, cancelled, failed}; requires_payment_method → requires_confirmation; requires_confirmation → {requires_action, requires_capture, processing, cancelled, failed}; requires_capture → {succeeded, cancelled}; processing → {succeeded, failed, cancelled}; succeeded/cancelled/failed are TERMINAL
status='succeeded' requires succeeded_at IS NOT NULL
status='cancelled' requires cancelled_at IS NOT NULL
status='succeeded' → a Payment row exists referencing this intent (atomic — same DB transaction)
status='requires_action' requires next_action_type IS NOT NULL
client_secret encrypted at rest with a key distinct from provider_reference_id's key
amount_cents + currency + capture_method + confirmation_method immutable after create
hard-deletion FORBIDDEN — intents retain 90 days (transient state; success → creates payment; failure → audit only)
